blocks.body: Past Continuous: Negatives — wasn't / weren't + verb-ing for negatives in the past.

{"level":"A2","intro":"In Past Continuous negatives, we use was not / were not or the short forms wasn't / weren't and keep the verb-ing form.","rules":[{"label":"wasn't / weren't","color":"emerald","text":"Remember the short forms for everyday English.","example":"I wasn't sleeping. / They weren't working."},{"label":"Keep -ing","color":"blue","text":"After was / were, the main verb stays in the -ing form.","example":"She wasn't reading. / We weren't waiting."},{"label":"Same idea, different subject","color":"amber","text":"Use wasn't with I / he / she / it and weren't with you / we / they.","example":"He wasn't cooking. / They weren't cooking."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic patterns","intro":"After was / were, keep the verb with -ing.","items":[{"label":"I / he / she / it","title":"was not / wasn't + verb-ing","subtitle":"I wasn't listening. / She wasn't sleeping."},{"label":"you / we / they","title":"were not / weren't + verb-ing","subtitle":"We weren't waiting. / They weren't coming."},{"label":"Full form","title":"was not / were not","subtitle":"He was not working. / They were not talking."},{"label":"Contrast","title":"was reading → wasn't reading","subtitle":"In a negative sentence, -ing stays."}]}

{"title":"2. Why you need negatives","sections":[{"label":"The action was not in progress","color":"emerald","description":"Show that an action was not happening at that moment in the past.","examples":[{"en":"I wasn't sleeping at 11 p.m.","ua":"The action was not happening then."},{"en":"They weren't waiting outside.","ua":"A negative action in progress."}]},{"label":"Contrast","color":"blue","description":"Compare what was happening and what was not happening.","examples":[{"en":"She was cooking, but she wasn't listening to music.","ua":"One action was happening, another one was not."},{"en":"We were studying, but we weren't writing notes.","ua":"A clear contrast."}]},{"label":"One be form for each subject group","color":"amber","description":"Do not mix up was and were.","examples":[{"en":"He wasn't working.","ua":"wasn't with he"},{"en":"They weren't working.","ua":"weren't with they"}]}]}

{"title":"3. Affirmative and negative","intro":"Compare the positive and negative patterns.","rows":[{"en":"I was reading at 9 p.m.","ua":"I wasn't reading at 9 p.m.","note":"was → wasn't"},{"en":"She was waiting for the bus.","ua":"She wasn't waiting for the bus.","note":"keep -ing"},{"en":"They were working at that time.","ua":"They weren't working at that time.","note":"were → weren't"}],"warning":"Do not say She wasn't read or They wasn't waiting."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Dropping -ing in a negative sentence.","wrong":"❌ He wasn't work at that moment.","right":"✅ He wasn't working at that moment."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using wasn't with they / we / you.","wrong":"❌ They wasn't sleeping.","right":"✅ They weren't sleeping."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Making a negative sentence without be.","wrong":"❌ We not waiting when she called.","right":"✅ We weren't waiting when she called."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["A Past Continuous negative = wasn't / weren't + verb-ing.","Use wasn't with I / he / she / it.","Use weren't with you / we / they.","In a negative sentence, the -ing form stays."]}
